title: Landslide destroys home in Ashikaga, trapping elderly couple
summary: Heavy rains triggered a landslide that buried a house in Tochigi city; emergency teams search for two missing elderly people.

title: Escalation of US-Iran conflict sends oil prices to one-month highs
summary: A month after signing an agreement, military attacks intensify and crude oil rises to $82 per barrel, with fears over supply from the Gulf.

title: Trump proposes tariffs to cover costs of smoke from Canadian wildfires
summary: The US president suggests that additional tariffs will offset the impact of air pollution affecting several US regions.

title: Reform of the Imperial House Law and extension of the Diet dominate political debate in Japan
summary: The government pushes changes to imperial succession with adoption options, while the opposition criticizes the process and the Diet extends its session to address the issue.

title: Fear of Chinese artificial intelligence triggers sharp drop in US and global stock markets
summary: The advancement of the Chinese 'Kimi' model triggers massive sell-offs of tech stocks, dragging down the Nasdaq and Dow Jones, and affecting Asian markets.

title: Japan enacts controversial law punishing desecration of national flag
summary: The new legislation divides opinion: while the government defends it, experts and some opposition members warn about its ambiguity and potential use to limit freedom of expression.

title: World Cup nears its conclusion with fight for third place and visit of Spanish prime minister
summary: France and England prepare for the bronze match, the race for the Golden Boot continues, and the Spanish prime minister plans to attend the final.

title: Yen holds around 162 yen to the dollar amid expectations of Bank of Japan intervention
summary: The Japanese currency stabilizes after recent weakness, but traders remain cautious about possible official action to stem the depreciation.

title: Osaka Expo electric buses have 832 defects during operation
summary: An Osaka Metro report reveals that the vehicles, purchased for over 9 billion yen, suffered multiple technical failures that call the project's safety into question.

title: Andy Burnham, Labour politician with links to Osaka, poised to become next UK prime minister
summary: Known as the 'King of the North', Burnham embodies opposition to Thatcherism and has a special connection with the Japanese city of Osaka.

title: Japan's maglev train project faces delays and demands clarity on timeline
summary: Work on the most difficult tunnel for the maglev reaches record depths, but after a decade of delays, there are calls for a realistic schedule.

title: Victims of wrongful convictions and their families demand reform of sentence review law
summary: After cases of innocent people wrongly imprisoned, there are calls for legal change that ensures faster and fairer reviews to prevent miscarriages of justice.

title: Ruling party proposes tightening rules against activist shareholders to protect market
summary: The initiative seeks to prevent interference considered abusive by activist shareholders, in an attempt to maintain confidence in the financial system.

title: Tokyo Stock Exchange plunges more than 4,100 points in day of heavy selling
summary: The Nikkei index suffers one of its biggest daily drops, dragged down by massive selling of Japanese stocks and uncertainty in global markets.

title: Seven years after arson attack on Kyoto Animation, families cry out for justice
summary: Relatives of the victims of the deadly 2019 arson attack urge authorities to resolve the case of the death row convict and close the judicial process.

title: Kim Jong-un meets Chinese senior official Wang Huning to strengthen bilateral alliance
summary: On the 65th anniversary of the North Korea-China friendship treaty, both leaders reaffirm their strategic cooperation and opposition to external pressures.

title: Xi Jinping offers to mediate peace between Thailand and Cambodia
summary: The Chinese president expresses his willingness to play a constructive role in resolving conflicts in the region, during meetings with the prime ministers of both countries.

title: Israeli parliament dissolves and general elections called for October
summary: Political instability and growing discontent with Netanyahu's government lead to another legislative dissolution, with an uncertain outcome.

title: Apple temporarily regains top spot as world's most valuable company, surpassing NVIDIA
summary: The Cupertino company sees a rebound in its market capitalization driven by the strength of its services, overtaking the chipmaker.

title: European Union proposes easing carbon market rules to relieve industry
summary: The European Commission presents a proposal to flexibilize the emissions trading system, in response to complaints from industrial sectors about high costs.
